0);\n38\t    assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 1);\n39\t    assert!(scope.parent_key.is_none());\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t#[test]\n43\tfn test_scope_stack_with_different_base_indents() {\n44\t    let stack_2 = test_scope_stack_reset_clears_all_scopes() {\n53\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n54\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"first\".to_string()));\n55\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"second\".to_string()));\n56\t    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 3).unwrap();\n57\t\n58\t    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n59\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n60\t\n61\t test_clear_keys_removes_all_keys() {\n109\t    let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n110\t    scope.add_key(\"key1\");\n111\t    scope.add_key(\"key2\");\n112\t    scope.add_key(\"key3\");\n113\t    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 3);\n114\t\n115\t    scope.clear_keys();\n116\t    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n117\t    assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n118\t}\n119\t\n120\t#[test]\n121\tfn test_stack_add_key_in_different_scopes() {\n122\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n123\t\n124\t    // Add to root scope\n125\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n126\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n127\t\n128\t    // Enter nested scope\n129\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n130\t\n131\t    // Same key should be OK in different scope\n132\t    stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).unwrap();\n133\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n134\t\n135\t    // Verify root scope still has its key\n136\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"host\"));\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t#[test]\n140\tfn test_stack_add_key_duplicate_in_current_scope() {\n141\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n142\t    stack.add_key(\"key\", 1).unwrap();\n143\t\n144\t    let result = stack.add_key(\"key\", 2);\n145\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n146\t\n147\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n148\t    assert_eq!(error.key, \"key\");\n149\t    assert_eq!(error.first_line, 0); // Root scope starts at line 0\n150\t    assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 2);\n151\t}\n152\t\n153\t#[test]\n154\tfn test_contains_key_in_any_scope() {\n155\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n156\t\n157\t    stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).unwrap();\n158\t\n159\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n160\t    stack.add_key(\"level1_key\", 3).unwrap();\n161\t\n162\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n163\t    stack.add_key(\"level2_key\", 5).unwrap();\n164\t\n165\t    // Current scope\n166\t    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"level2_key\"));\n167\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level1_key\"));\n168\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"));\n169\t\n170\t    // Any scope\n171\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level2_key\"));\n172\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level1_key\"));\n173\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"root_key\"));\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// =============================================================================\n177\t// Scope Transition Tests\n178\t// =============================================================================\n179\t\n180\t#[test]\n181\tfn test_enter_scope_creates_new_scope() {\n182\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n183\t    let initial_depth = stack.depth();\n184\t\n185\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n186\t\n187\t    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), initial_depth + 1);\n188\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n189\t    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t#[test]\n193\tfn test_enter_nested_scopes() {\n194\t    let mut stack = assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2.level3\");\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t#[test]\n207\tfn test_exit_to_scope_removes_deeper_scopes() {\n208\t    let mut stack = mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n226\t\n227\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n228\t\n229\t    // Exit to a scope that doesn't exist (level 3 when we only have 0 and 2)\n230\t    stack.exit_to_scope(3);\n231\t\n232\t    // Should create a fallback scope at level 3\n233\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 3);\n234\t}\n235\t\n236\t#[test]\n237\tfn test_scope_reuse_at_same_level() {\n238\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n239\t\n240\t    // Enter first scope at level 2\n241\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"first\".to_string()));\n242\t    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 2).unwrap();\n243\t    stack.add_key(\"key2\", 3).unwrap();\n244\t\n245\t    // Exit back to root\n246\t    stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n247\t\n248\t    // Enter second scope at same level 2\n249\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"second\".to_string()));\n250\t\n251\t    // Should have cleared keys from first scope\n252\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n253\t    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n254\t\n255\t    // Same key should be OK (different scope)\n256\t    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 6).unwrap();\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t#[test]\n260\tfn test_get_scope_at_level() {\n261\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n262\t\n263\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n264\t    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(2).is_some());\n269\t    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(4).is_some());\n270\t    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_some());\n271\t\n272\t    // Level that doesn't exist\n273\t    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(8).is_none());\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\t// =============================================================================\n277\t// Sequence Scope stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n283\t\n284\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n285\t\n286\t    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n287\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n288\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(1));\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t#[test]\n292\tfn test_sequence_item_ids_are_unique() {\n293\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n294\t\n295\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n296\t    let id1 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n297\t\n298\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n299\t    let id2 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n300\t\n301\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n302\t    let id3 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n303\t\n304\t    assert_eq!(id1, Some(1));\n305\t    assert_eq!(id2, Some(2));\n306\t    assert_eq!(id3, Some(3));\n307\t}\n308\t\n309\t#[test]\n310\tfn test_sequence_scope_clears_previous_keys() {\n311\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n312\t\n313\t    // First sequence item\n314\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n315\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n316\t    stack.add_key(\"value\", 3).unwrap();\n317\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n318\t\n319\t    // Second sequence item (should clear keys)\n320\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n321\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n322\t\n323\t    // Keys can be reused in new sequence item\n324\t    stack.add_key(\"name\", 6).unwrap();\n325\t    stack.add_key(\"value\", 7).unwrap();\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t#[test]\n329\tfn test_duplicate_within_sequence_scope_detected() {\n330\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n331\t\n332\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, test_sequence_scopes_at_different_levels() {\n341\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n342\t\n343\t    // Regular scope\n344\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n345\t\n346\t    // Sequence scope within regular scope\n347\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n348\t    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n349\t assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n354\t\n355\t    // Enter another sequence scope\n356\t    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 4);\n357\t    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n358\t    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(2));\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t#[test]\n362\tfn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n363\t    let mut stack = \").unwrap();\n461\t    match ctx {\n462\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n463\t            assert_eq!(key, \"key\");\n464\t            assert_eq!(value, \"value\");\n465\t =============================================================================\n573\t\n574\t#[test]\n575\tfn test_duplicate_key_error_creation() {\n576\t    let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n577\t        \"test_key\".to_string(),\n578\t        \"scope.path\".to_string(),\n579\t        5,\n580\t        10\n581\t    );\n582\t\n583\t    assert_eq!(error.key, \"test_key\");\n584\t test_duplicate_key_error_message() {\n591\t    let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n592\t        \"host\".to_string(),\n593\t        \"services.web\".to_string(),\n594\t        3,\n595\t        7\n596\t    );\n597\t\n598\t    let message = error.message();\n599\t    assert!(message.contains(\"Line 7\"));\n600\t    assert!(message.contains(\"duplicate key\"));\n601\t    assert!(message.contains(\"host\"));\n602\t    assert!(message.contains(\"services.web\"));\n603\t    assert!(message.contains(\"Line 3\"));\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t#[test]\n607\tfn test_duplicate_key_error_display() {\n608\t    let error = DuplicateKeyError::new(\n609\t        \"port\".to_string(),\n610\t        \"config\".to_string(),\n611\t        1,\n612\t        5\n613\t    );\n614\t\n615\t    let display = format!(\"{}\", error);\n616\t    assert!(display.contains(\"Line 5\"));\n617\t    assert!(display.contains(\"port\"));\n618\t =============================================================================\n// Scope Creation and Initialization Tests\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_creation_with_all_fields() {\n    let scope = Scope::new(4, 10, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 4);\n    assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 10);\n    assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n assert_eq!(stack_4.base_indent(), 4);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_stack_reset_clears_all_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"first\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"second\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n scope.add_key(\"test\");\n    assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"test\"), true);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_add_multiple_keys() {\n    let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    scope.add_key(\"key1\");\n    scope.add_key(\"key3\");\n    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 3);\n\n    scope.clear_keys();\n    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n    assert!(!scope.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_stack_add_key_in_different_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Add to root scope\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n\n    // Enter nested scope\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n    // Same key should be OK in different scope\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).unwrap();\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n\n    // Verify root scope still has its key\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"host\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_stack_add_key_duplicate_in_current_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.add_key(\"key\", 1).unwrap();\n\n    let result = stack.add_key(\"key\", 2);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert_eq!(error.key, \"key\");\n    assert_eq!(error.first_line, 0); // Root scope starts at line 0\n    assert_eq!(error.duplicate_line, 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_contains_key_in_any_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).unwrap();\n\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"level1_key\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"level2_key\", 5).unwrap();\n\n    // Current scope\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"level2_key\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"level1_key\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"root_key\"));\n\n    // Any scope\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level2_key\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"level1_key\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"root_key\"));\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_enter_scope_creates_new_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    let initial_depth = assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"parent\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_enter_nested_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2\");\n\n    stack.enter_scope(6, assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_exit_to_nonexistent_scope_creates_fallback() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n\n    // Exit to a scope that doesn't exist (level 3 when we only have 0 and 2)\n    stack.exit_to_scope(3);\n\n    // Should create a fallback scope at level 3\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 3);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_reuse_at_same_level() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Enter first scope at level 2\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"first\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"key2\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Exit back to root\n    stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n    // Enter second scope at same level 2\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"second\".to_string()));\n\n    // Should have cleared keys from first scope\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n\n    // Same key should be OK (different scope)\n    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 6).unwrap();\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_get_scope_at_level() {\n    let mut stack = Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(0).is_some());\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(2).is_some());\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(4).is_some());\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(6).is_some());\n\n    // Level that doesn't exist\n    assert!(stack.get_scope_at_level(8).is_none());\n}\n\n// assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(1));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_item_ids_are_unique() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    let id1 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n    let id2 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    let id3 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    assert_eq!(id1, Some(1));\n    assert_eq!(id2, Some(2));\n    assert_eq!(id3, Some(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_clears_previous_keys() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // First sequence item\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 3).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n\n    // Second sequence item (should clear keys)\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n\n    // Keys can be reused in new sequence item\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 6).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 7).unwrap();\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_duplicate_within_sequence_scope_detected() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n\n    let result = stack.add_key(\"name\", 3);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scopes_at_different_levels() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Regular scope\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n\n    // Sequence scope within regular scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(1));\n\n    // Exit back to regular scope\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n    // Enter another sequence scope\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 4);\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(2));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Regular scope with keys\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"config\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"timeout\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"retries\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // Sequence scope (should not affect regular scope)\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 4);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 5).unwrap();\n\n    // Exit to regular scope\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Regular scope keys should still be present\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"timeout\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"retries\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"name\")); // name was in sequence scope\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Scope Path Generation Tests\n// \"services\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_path_multiple_levels() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2.level3\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_path_after_exit() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2\");\n\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_scope_path_sequence_context() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 2);\n\n    // Sequence scopes don't have parent keys, so path stays at \"items\"\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"items\");\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// KeyContext Extraction Tests\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_inline_scalar_key_context() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"host: localhost\").unwrap();\n    match ctx {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n            assert_eq!(key, \"host\");\n            assert_eq!(value, \"localhost\");\n        }\n        _ => panic!(\"Expected InlineScalar\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_parent_mapping_key_context() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"services:\").unwrap();\n    match ctx {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\n            assert_eq!(key, \"services\");\n        }\n        _ => panic!(\"Expected ParentMapping\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_with_spaces_after_colon() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"key:   value  \").unwrap();\n    match ctx {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n            assert_eq!(key, \"key\");\n            assert_eq!(value, \"value\");\n        }\n        _ => panic!(\"Expected InlineScalar\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_with_special_chars() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"my-key: value\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"my-key\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_with_underscores() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"my_key: value\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"my_key\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_no_colon() {\n    assert!(extract_key_context(\"no colon here\").is_none());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_empty_key() {\n    assert!(extract_key_context(\": value\").is_none());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_flow_style_braces() {\n    assert!(extract_key_context(\"{key: value}\").is_none());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_flow_style_brackets() {\n    assert!(extract_key_context(\"[item1, item2]\").is_none());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_with_tabs_in_value() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"key:\\tvalue\").unwrap();\n    match ctx {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n            assert_eq!(key, \"key\");\n            assert_eq!(value, \"value\");\n        }\n        _ => panic!(\"Expected InlineScalar\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_extract_key_context_quoted_value() {\n    let ctx = extract_key_context(\"key: \\\"quoted value\\\"\").unwrap();\n    match ctx {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\n            assert_eq!(key, \"key\");\n            assert_eq!(value, \"\\\"quoted value\\\"\");\n        }\n        _ => panic!(\"Expected InlineScalar\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_key_context_is_parent_key() {\n    let inline = extract_key_context(\"key: value\").unwrap();\n    assert!(!inline.is_parent_key());\n    assert!(inline.is_inline_scalar());\n\n    let parent = extract_key_context(\"parent:\").unwrap();\n    assert!(parent.is_parent_key());\n    assert!(!parent.is_inline_scalar());\n}\n\n// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"  key: value\"), 2);\n    assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"    key: value\"), 4);\n    assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"      key: value\"), 6);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_leading_whitespace_length_tabs() {\n    assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"\\tkey: value\"), 1);\n    assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\"\\t\\tkey: value\"), 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_leading_whitespace_length_mixed() {\n    // Tabs count as 1 each\n    assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\" \\t key: value\"), 3);\n}\n\n// \"host\".to_string(),\n        \"services.web\".to_string(),\n        3,\n        7\n    );\n\n    let message = error.message();\n    assert!(message.contains(\"Line 7\"));\n scope.add_key(\"key1\");\n    scope.add_key(\"key2\");\n\n    let display = format!(\"{}\", scope);\n    assert!(display.contains(\"Scope\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"indent=2\"));\n assert!(display.contains(\"current_path=level1.level2\"));\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Stress Tests\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_many_keys_in_single_scope() {\n    let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n\n    // Add 1000 unique keys\n    for i in 0..1000 {\n        let key = format!(\"key_{}\", i);\n        assert_eq!(scope.add_key(&key), false);\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 1000);\n\n    // Verify all keys exist\n    for i in 0..1000 {\n        let key = format!(\"key_{}\", i);\n        assert!(scope.contains_key(&key));\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_deep_nesting_stack() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Create 20 levels of nesting\n    let mut expected_path = String::new();\n    for i in 1..=20 {\n        let key = format!(\"level{}\", i);\n        if !expected_path.is_empty() {\n            expected_path.push('.');\n        }\n        expected_path.push_str(&key);\n\n        stack.enter_scope(i * 2, i as usize, Some(key.clone()));\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 21); // 20 + root\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), expected_path);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_many_sibling_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Create 100 sibling scopes\n    for i in 1..=100 {\n        let key = format!(\"sibling{}\", i);\n\n        // Exit to root, then enter new sibling\n        stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n        stack.enter_scope(2, i as usize, Some(key.clone()));\n\n        // Add same key to each sibling (should be OK)\n        stack.add_key(\"shared_key\", i as usize).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Should only have root + current scope\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_many_sequence_items() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Create 100 sequence items\n    for i in 1..=100 {\n        stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, i as usize);\n\n        // Each item should have unique ID\n        assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(i));\n\n        // Same key should be OK in different sequence items\n        stack.add_key(\"name\", i as usize).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id, Some(100));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_complex_realistic_structure() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Simulate complex YAML structure\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n\n    // First sibling scope (web)\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 3).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"port\", 4).unwrap();\n    // Verify keys are in current scope\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"port\"));\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Second sibling scope (database) - same keys should be OK\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 5, Some(\"database\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 6).unwrap(); // Same key, different scope - OK\n    stack.add_key(\"port\", 7).unwrap();  // Same key, different scope - OK\n    // Verify keys are in current scope\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"port\"));\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n\n    // Third scope with nested sequences\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 8, Some(\"cache\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(6, 9);\n    stack.add_key(\"server\", 10).unwrap();\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(6, 11);\n    stack.add_key(\"server\", 12).unwrap(); // Same key, different seq item - OK\n\n    // Verify we're in the cache scope with sequences\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.cache\");\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Edge Cases\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_empty_key_string() {\n    let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    // Empty string is a valid stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n295\t\n296\t    // Second sibling\n297\t    stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"sibling2\".to_string()));\n298\t    // Keys from first sibling should not be present\n299\t \").unwrap();\n551\t    assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\n552\t    assert_eq!(ctx.key_name(), \"timeout\");\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\t#[test]\n556\tfn value1\n833\tkey2: value2\n834\tkey3: value3\n835\t\"#;\n836\t\n837\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n838\t\n839\t    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n840\t        let localhost\n861\t    port: 8080\n862\t  database:\n863\t    host: db.example.com\n864\t    port: 5432\n865\t\"#;\n866\t\n867\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n868\t\n869\t    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n870\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n871\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n872\t            continue;\n873\t        }\n874\t\n875\t        let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n876\t\n877\t        // Handle scope transitions\n878\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n879\t        match indent.cmp(&stack.current_indent()) {\n880\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n881\t                // Entering deeper scope\n882\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n883\t                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n884\t                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n885\t                        stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num + 1, Some(ctx.key_name().to_string()));\n886\t                    }\n887\t                }\n888\t            }\n889\t            Ordering::Less => {\n890\t                // Exiting to parent scope\n891\t                stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n892\t                // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n893\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n894\t                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n895\t ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n898\t                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n899\t                    }\n900\t                }\n901\t            }\n902\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n903\t                // Same scope - check for keys\n904\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n905\t                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n906\t                        // This is a sibling scope at same indent level\n907\t                        stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n908\t                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n909\t                        stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num + 1, Some(ctx.key_name().to_string()));\n910\t                    } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n911\t                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n912\t                    }\n913\t                }\n914\t            }\n915\t        }\n916\t    }\n917\t\n918\t    // Should have keys in multiple scopes without duplicates\n919\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"services\"));\n920\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"web\"));\n921\t    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"database\"));\n922\t}\n923\t\n924\t#[test]\n925\tfn test_sequence_yaml_structure() {\n926\t    let yaml = r#\"\n927\titems:\n928\t  - name: item1\n929\t    value: 100\n930\t  - name: item2\n931\t    value: 200\n932\t  - name: item3\n933\t    value: 300\n934\t\"#;\n935\t\n936\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n937\t\n938\t    stack.enter_scope(2, test_same_key_in_different_scopes_succeeds() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Add to root\n    stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).unwrap();\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n\n assert!(stack.contains_key(\"host\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_keys_isolated_between_siblings() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // First sibling\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"sibling1\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"key2\", 3).unwrap();\n    stack.exit_to_scope(0);\n\n    // Second sibling\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"sibling2\".to_string()));\n    // Keys from first sibling should not be present\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"key2\"));\n\n    // Can add same keys to second sibling\n    stack.add_key(\"key1\", 6).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"key2\", 7).unwrap();\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"key1\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key(\"key2\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_contains_key_in_any_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.add_key(\"root_key\", 1).unwrap();\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"level1\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"nested_key\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"root_key\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"nested_key\"));\n\n    stack.enter_scope(4, 4, Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    // From deep scope, can still find keys in parent scopes\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"root_key\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"nested_key\"));\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Sequence Scope Operations\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_enter_sequence_scope() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_indent(), 2);\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().in_sequence_context, true);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_unique_ids() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    let id1 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 3);\n    let id2 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    let id3 = stack.current_scope_ref().sequence_item_id;\n\n    assert_eq!(id1, Some(1));\n    assert_eq!(id2, Some(2));\n    assert_eq!(id3, Some(3));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_scope_clears_previous_keys() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 3).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n\n    // New sequence item should clear keys\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 0);\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"name\"));\n    assert!(!stack.contains_key(\"value\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_same_key_in_different_sequence_items() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 3).unwrap();\n\n    // New sequence item - same keys should be OK\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 6).unwrap();\n    stack.add_key(\"value\", 7).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_duplicate_within_sequence_item_fails() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 1);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 2).unwrap();\n\n    let result = stack.add_key(\"name\", 3);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_mixed_regular_and_sequence_scopes() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Regular scope\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n    stack.add_key(\"item1\", 2).unwrap();\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n    // Sequence scope within regular\n    stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, 3);\n    stack.add_key(\"name\", 4).unwrap();\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n\n    // Exit back to regular scope\n    stack.exit_to_scope(2);\n    assert!(!stack.in_sequence_context());\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Scope Path Generation\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"level3\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"level1.level2.level3\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn Some(\"level2\".to_string()));\n    assert_ne!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"\");\n\n    stack.reset();\n    assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"\");\n}\n\n// Some(\"l2\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 3);\n\n    stack.enter_scope(6, 3, Some(\"l3\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 4);\n\n    stack.reset();\n    assert_eq!(stack.depth(), 1);\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Integration with YAML Parsing\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_simple_yaml_structure() {\n    let yaml = r#\"\nkey1: value1\nkey2: value2\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n            if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                let _ = stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(stack.current_scope_ref().key_count(), 3);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_nested_yaml_structure() {\n    let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let indent = get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n\n        // Handle scope transitions\n        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n        match indent.cmp(&stack.current_indent()) {\n            Ordering::Greater => {\n                // Entering deeper scope\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n                        stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num + 1, Some(ctx.key_name().to_string()));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Ordering::Less => {\n                // Exiting to parent scope\n                stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n                        stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num + 1, Some(ctx.key_name().to_string()));\n                    } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Ordering::Equal => {\n                // Same scope - check for keys\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                        // This is a sibling scope at same indent level\n                        stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n                        stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num + 1, Some(ctx.key_name().to_string()));\n                    } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                        stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1).unwrap();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Should have keys in multiple scopes without duplicates\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"services\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"web\"));\n    assert!(stack.contains_key_in_any_scope(\"database\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_sequence_yaml_structure() {\n    let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n  - name: item2\n    value: 200\n  - name: item3\n    value: 300\n\"#;\n\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    stack.enter_scope(2, 2, Some(\"items\".to_string()));\n\n    for (line_num, line) in yaml.lines().enumerate() {\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Detect sequence items\n        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n            stack.enter_sequence_scope(4, line_num + 1);\n        } else if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n            if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                let _ = stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num + 1);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Should have processed sequence items successfully\n    assert!(stack.in_sequence_context());\n}\n","numLines":959,"startLine":1,"totalLines":959}}}

Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1024}}}
